name: Honda Accord (10th Gen)
engine: 1.5L turbo I4 (L15BE), 192 hp — 2.0L turbo I4 (K20C4), 252 hp on Sport 2.0T
transmission: CVT (1.5T) · 10-speed automatic (2.0T) · 6-speed manual offered on the Sport
reliability: 9/10 — a genuinely strong record and a frequent reliability-award winner. The documented items are software-era infotainment quirks on early cars and the 2020 Denso low-pressure fuel-pump recall, which was industry-wide across many brands, not Accord-specific. Both turbo fours have held up well; NHTSA volume is moderate for a top-selling sedan.
cost_to_own: Insurance: Average · Maintenance ~$400/yr · Strong resale; note the 2.0T asks for premium fuel while the 1.5T runs on regular
popular_mods: Hondata flash on the 2.0T — it shares the Civic Type R's K20C engine family, so there's a real, proven tuning path
All-weather liners and tasteful wheels with quality tires
A second set of winter tires for cold-climate owners
Suspension refresh (OEM-plus dampers/springs) on higher-mileage cars
Honest take: the 1.5T is a sensible commuter; the manual 2.0T Sport is the quiet enthusiast pick and where any money is best spent

generations.10th Gen (2018–2022): A turbo-four lineup that replaced the old V6: 1.5L turbo standard, 252hp 2.0L turbo (with a 10-speed automatic, and a 6-speed manual early on) up top. Roomy, refined, and sharp-handling for a family sedan, with Honda Sensing standard.
generations.The V6 is gone: This generation dropped the naturally-aspirated J35 V6 entirely — see our separate Accord V6 page for the 2013–2017 car buyers seek out for that engine.
generations.Replaced by the 11th Gen (2023+): The 2023 redesign kept the 1.5T and added a stronger hybrid, leaning further toward efficiency.

common_issues: Early infotainment and driver-assist software quirks (improved with updates)
2020 Denso low-pressure fuel-pump recall — industry-wide, affected many makes including Honda
Some reports of start-stop AGM battery wear on short-trip cars
More tire and road noise than a luxury sedan — a refinement gripe, not a fault
maintenance_tips: Run premium fuel in the 2.0T as Honda specifies; the 1.5T is happy on regular
Keep infotainment/control software updated on early cars
Service the 10-speed automatic (2.0T) and the CVT (1.5T) fluids on schedule
Have the fuel-pump recall confirmed complete on a 2018–2020 car
